title: (No AI) Roelof Botha joins SpaceX’s board of directors

title: (No AI) Midjourney goes from generating cat images to full-body ultrasound scans
author: Richard Lawler
content:
![Midjourney Medical 3D scans of a “phantom” body](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/ultrasound_phantom_segmentation-b254a71d083eb5c9.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

“A scan of an imaging phantom, segmented to validate how cleanly structures separate under controlled conditions.“ | Image: Midjourney Medical

Midjourney CEO David Holz just showed off the company's first hardware product and plans to build a San Francisco spa, which he admitted is a bit different from the "cat pictures" produced by its AI image generator. Dubbed [The Midjourney Scanner](https://www.midjourney.com/medical), it's an ultrasound-based full-body scanner that uses a ring of sensors to capture vertical slices of the inside of your body, looking at the composition of your muscle, fat, bone, and organs to start. Holz said ideally, you could do this once a year or every single day, as it "aims for image quality comparable to MRI in many ways."

He mentioned that one way he'd like to use it would be to see how h …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/952011/midjourney-medical-ai-ultrasound-scan)

“A scan of an imaging phantom, segmented to validate how cleanly structures separate under controlled conditions.“ | Image: Midjourney Medical	

Midjourney CEO David Holz just showed off the company's first hardware product and plans to build a San Francisco spa, which he admitted is a bit different from the "cat pictures" produced by its AI image generator. Dubbed The Midjourney Scanner, it's an ultrasound-based full-body scanner that uses a ring of sensors to capture vertical slices of the inside of your body, looking at the composition of your muscle, fat, bone, and organs to start. Holz said ideally, you could do this once a year or every single day, as it "aims for image quality comparable to MRI in many ways."
He mentioned that one way he'd like to use it would be to see how h …

title: (No AI) Anthropic got hit by export rules nobody understands
author: Robert Hart
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/STKB364_CLAUDE_2_A_3800fc.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Anthropic has spent much of this week fighting to get its newest AI models back online after the Trump administration [abruptly ordered](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/949553/anthropic-fable-5-mythos-5-government-national-security) the company to cut access for all foreign nationals, including users inside the US and its own employees, forcing Anthropic to block access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for everyone.

> "To my knowledge, this is the first time US export controls have been used to control access to an AI model in this way."

The Trump administration has not publicly explained the legal basis for the order, but in a statement on its website, Anthropic [said](https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access) the government cited "national security authorities" to justify "an export con …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/951703/anthropic-shutdown-export-controls)

title: (No AI) The UK Will Scan Asylum-Seekers’ Faces for Age Checks—Despite Knowing the Tech Is Flawed

creator: Louise Matsakis, Maxwell Zeff
title: (No AI) The Korean Telecom Giant at the Center of Anthropic’s Mythos Controversy
dc:creator: Louise Matsakis, Maxwell Zeff
content: Days before Anthropic took its most advanced AI models offline, the White House ordered the company to revoke SK Telecom’s access to Claude Mythos over claims of alleged ties to China.
contentSnippet: Days before Anthropic took its most advanced AI models offline, the White House ordered the company to revoke SK Telecom’s access to Claude Mythos over claims of alleged ties to China.
